title: Bitter Norwegian Black Pepper Gratin
description: A romantic Norwegian dish featuring black pepper, perfect for a cold winter evening. The bitterness of the pepper is balanced by the creaminess of the potatoes, making it a unique and satisfying meal.

ingredients.name: large potatoes
ingredients.notes: preferably high-starch potatoes like Russet or Idaho

instructions: Peel the potatoes and slice them thinly into a large bowl.
Microwave the sliced potatoes for 4 minutes to partially cook them.
In a separate pan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat.
Add the black peppercorns to the pan and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the flour to the pan and whisk to combine. Cook for 1 minute.
Slowly pour in the heavy cream, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes, until it thickens.
Stir in the Parmesan cheese, if using.
Add the cooked potatoes to the sauce and toss to combine.
Transfer the potato mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Dot the top of the potatoes with the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake for 30 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 15-20 minutes, until the top is golden brown.
Garnish with chopped parsley before serving.
Serve hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
